summary: Demand evidence agents completed, but readiness still has blockers.

demand_status.summary: Demand is not PRD-ready; missing slots, blockers, assumptions, or evidence remain.

triage.explanation: Fast route by default; no hard factual risk trigger was found.

problem: Existing YOLO harness boundary audit must detect tracked and untracked target-project mutations in git-backed and non-git projects.
status_quo: YOLO demand evidence dispatch exists in a dirty working tree and must be stress-tested with real Claude -p agents.
desired_outcome: Boundary snapshots cover git ls-files tracked/cached/other files plus fallback walks and exclude only the dispatch artifact root.
scope_out: Do not intentionally modify target project files during evidence gathering.
constraints: Full tools may be available; harness boundary audit must detect any target-project mutation.
acceptance_criteria: Boundary snapshots cover git ls-files tracked/cached/other files plus fallback walks and exclude only the dispatch artifact root.
risks: A full-tool agent could create a new target project file without readiness blockers.

blockers.message: explorer must complete with evidence and a result before cross_check PRD readiness.
verifier must complete with evidence and a result before cross_check PRD readiness.
explorer must complete with evidence and a result before cross_check PRD readiness.
verifier must complete with evidence and a result before cross_check PRD readiness.

protocol.objective: Find primary project facts for the requested demand without editing files.
protocol.prompt: Read only the relevant files, configs, logs, docs, and current implementation. Return claims with exact paths/lines or source summaries. Separate assumptions from facts.

actions.reason: Verify blockers and assumptions are not promoted to facts.

protocol.objective: Check whether gathered evidence is enough to support PRD readiness and acceptance criteria.
protocol.prompt: Verify that no assumption is presented as fact, evidence paths/sources are usable, and unresolved missing data is reflected as blockers or assumptions.

agent_results.claim: Evidence dispatch boundary snapshots cover git tracked/cached/other files via git ls-files with fallback walks and exclude the dispatch artifact root. The implementation exists and has basic test coverage, but has not been stress-tested with real Claude agents in full-tool mode.

evidence.summary: gitFiles() uses git ls-files -co with --exclude-standard to capture cached (tracked) and other (untracked) files while respecting .gitignore. Returns null on git failure.
evidence.why: Implements the git-backed file discovery part of the boundary snapshot requirement.

evidence.summary: walkFiles() recursively walks the filesystem as a fallback, excluding common ignore directories (.git, node_modules, dist, coverage, .next, .cache).
evidence.why: Implements the non-git fallback walk part of the boundary snapshot requirement.

evidence.summary: buildBoundarySnapshot() uses gitFiles() when available, falls back to walkFiles(), and excludes files within allowedRoots (the dispatch artifact root) from the snapshot.
evidence.why: Combines git discovery and fallback walks, and implements artifact root exclusion per the acceptance criteria.

evidence.summary: diffBoundarySnapshots() compares before/after snapshots and returns added/modified/deleted changes with file paths.
evidence.why: Detects mutations that violate the boundary constraint.

evidence.summary: Runtime builds boundary snapshot before agent execution with output_dir in allowed write roots.
evidence.why: Shows the artifact root is properly excluded from mutation detection.

evidence.summary: Runtime builds boundary snapshot after agents complete, diffs against before snapshot, and reports boundary violations in result.
evidence.why: Shows the complete boundary enforcement flow.

evidence.summary: Boundary violations become BOUNDARY_PROJECT_MUTATION blockers and set prd_ready=false.
evidence.why: Shows how boundary violations affect demand readiness.

evidence.summary: Test: git-backed boundary detects untracked project mutations. Creates a git repo, agent writes untracked-mutation.txt, test expects blocked status with violated boundary.
evidence.why: Evidence that git-backed boundary detection is tested.

evidence.summary: Test: non-git boundary fallback detects modified project files and ignores artifacts. Agent modifies src/existing.txt and writes to runtimeDir (allowed), test expects blocked for the modification but not the artifact.
evidence.why: Evidence that fallback walks work and artifact exclusion is tested.

evidence.summary: Test: boundary blocks project file mutation even when agent results pass. Agent writes unexpected-project-write.txt to project root, test expects blocked status.
evidence.why: Evidence that boundary enforcement works even when agent claims success.

evidence.summary: Evidence agent protocols define explorer and verifier roles with writes_code=false and boundary-controlled agent instruction.
evidence.why: Shows the architecture expects evidence agents to be read-only.

evidence.summary: executionPolicy sets agent_tool_profile to 'boundary' by default, with project_mutation: forbidden and allowed_write_roots containing only the output_dir.
evidence.why: Shows the default boundary constraint configuration.

evidence.summary: Agent prompt includes 'Boundary: do not modify target project files' and specifies the allowed artifact root.
evidence.why: Shows the instruction contract tells agents not to mutate project files.

risks: An agent could create a new project file (not in git ls-files) and the boundary would not detect it if the file is created within the allowed artifact root or if the fallback walk does not cover the location.
The git ls-files command does not include staged but uncommitted files in cached output; only files in the working tree are captured.
The boundary detection relies on file digests (SHA256); if an agent modifies a file and then restores it before boundary snapshot, the mutation would not be detected.
missing: Stress test with real Claude agent in full-tool mode to verify boundary holds under realistic conditions.
Test coverage for staged but uncommitted files to confirm they are included in boundary snapshots.
Test coverage for agents creating project files within the artifact root directory structure to verify current behavior.
Verification that the boundary works correctly when the project root is not a git repo but has nested .git directories in subdirectories.

result.notes: The boundary snapshot implementation covers the acceptance criteria with git ls-files for tracked/cached/other files, fallback walks for non-git projects, and exclusion of the dispatch artifact root. Test coverage exists for git-backed and non-git boundary detection. However, the demand status correctly identifies this as blocked by EVIDENCE_AGENT_RESULT_REQUIRED because real-world stress testing with full-tool Claude agents has not been performed.

agent_results.claim: The explorer's evidence accurately supports the claim that boundary snapshots exist with test coverage, but contains one incorrect fact and several unverified assumptions. The boundary implementation is real and mostly works as documented, but verification revealed factual errors in the risk assessment and gaps in test coverage that justify the blocked status.

evidence.summary: git ls-files uses -co flags: -c shows cached/staged files, -o shows untracked files. This directly contradicts explorer risk #2 about staged files not being included.
evidence.why: Verifies that the risk assessment about staged files is factually incorrect.

evidence.summary: Boundary snapshots are built before agent execution and after all agents complete, with diff comparison detecting added/modified/deleted files. Shows the complete enforcement flow.
evidence.why: Confirms the explorer's claim about the implementation structure.

evidence.summary: Test for git-backed boundary detection with untracked file mutations exists and verifies blocked status.
evidence.why: Confirms test coverage evidence for git-backed scenarios.

evidence.summary: Test for non-git fallback walks with artifact exclusion exists and verifies blocked status for modifications.
evidence.why: Confirms test coverage evidence for non-git scenarios.

evidence.summary: buildBoundarySnapshot excludes files within allowedRoots from the snapshot, which matches the acceptance criteria for artifact root exclusion.
evidence.why: Verifies the artifact exclusion implementation matches requirements.

assumptions: Agents creating files within the artifact root structure then moving them outside would not be detected by the boundary snapshot.
Agents modifying a file and restoring it before the boundary snapshot would not be detected.
The fallback walk handles nested .git directories correctly (no test coverage found).
Stress testing with real full-tool Claude agents would not reveal boundary violations (no evidence).
risks: The explorer incorrectly stated that git ls-files does not include staged files; the -c flag actually shows staged files, making this risk factually wrong.
Two identified risks are actually real vulnerabilities: file restoration attacks and artifact root race conditions.
Missing test coverage for edge cases means the boundary might fail in production scenarios.
The blocked status is justified because real-world validation has not been performed.
missing: Correction to risk assessment: git ls-files -c DOES include staged files in the cached output.
Test coverage for agents creating files in artifact root then moving them to project directories.
Test coverage for modify-then-restore attack scenarios.
Test coverage for nested .git directory handling in non-git projects.
Evidence that fallback walk handles subdirectories with their own .git directories correctly.
Stress test with real full-tool Claude agent to verify boundary holds under realistic conditions.

result.notes: Explorer evidence is mostly accurate with one factual error in risk assessment. The boundary implementation exists and matches the acceptance criteria. Test coverage confirms basic functionality. However, unverified assumptions about edge cases and missing stress testing justify the blocked status. The PRD should be updated to correct the incorrect fact about staged files and include the missing edge cases in risk acceptance criteria.
